title: Pyrus llangollenica
taxonomy: Pyrus llangollenica is classified within the family Rosaceae, specifically within the genus Pyrus, which includes other species of pears and related trees. It is a deciduous tree native to Wales, UK. The species is closely related to Pyrus communis, the common pear, and shares similar characteristics. The classification of Pyrus llangollenica is subject to ongoing research and taxonomic revisions. Further studies are needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of this species within the Pyrus genus.
uses: Pyrus llangollenica, a species of hawthorn tree, is native to the UK and has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. In folk medicine, extracts from Pyrus llangollenica have been used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
